quick_skim.title: Generative Modeling on Metric Graphs via Neural Optimal Transport
quick_skim.abstract: We introduce, to our knowledge, the first deep generative modeling framework for probability distributions continuously supported on compact metric graphs. Given source and target measures on a metric graph, our method embeds the graph into a smooth ambient space, solves an entropic Kantorovich problem via a neural semidual parameterization, and projects generated samples back onto the original graph. We study two embedded geometries: an extrinsic Euclidean realization and the intrinsic tropical Abel--Jacobi embedding into the Jacobian torus. In both cases, the resulting generator is graph-supported by construction. We prove that, in the joint limit of increasing neural expressivity, the learned generator converges weakly to a valid transport coupling between the original graph measures. Empirically, across a range of geometrically distinct graphs, our method matches or improves upon heuristic transport baselines based on discrete graph OT, while scaling more favorably. Finally, we demonstrate scalability on real-world urban mobility data by training our model on one million Uber pickup locations in Manhattan, New York City.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: neural optimal transport between source and target measures on metric graphs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 度量图上源和目标测度之间的神经最优传输
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 度量图上源和目标测度之间的神经最优传输
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 度量图上源和目标测度之间的神经最优传输
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes first deep generative framework for distributions on metric graphs using neural optimal transport, with convergence guarantees.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对度量图上连续分布的高效生成问题，提出了首个深度生成框架。通过将图嵌入光滑空间，采用神经半对偶参数化求解熵正则化最优传输问题，并将生成样本投影回原图。理论证明，在神经网络表达足够强的极限下，学习到的生成器弱收敛到有效的传输耦合。实验表明该方法在合成和真实图数据上均能生成高质量样本。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对度量图上连续分布的高效生成问题，提出了首个深度生成框架。通过将图嵌入光滑空间，采用神经半对偶参数化求解熵正则化最优传输问题，并将生成样本投影回原图。理论证明，在神经网络表达足够强的极限下，学习到的生成器弱收敛到有效的传输耦合。实验表明该方法在合成和真实图数据上均能生成高质量样本。
